desc: Tokyo is a strong base for exploring Japan with distinct seasonal trade-offs between weather comfort, crowd levels, and pricing.
hls: Seasonal climate profile benchmarked for Tokyo
Balanced mix of culture, food, and day-trip potential
Works for both short breaks and longer stays
Monthly data supports weather-first trip timing

todo.name: Walk central Tokyo
todo.desc: Use a half-day orientation walk through the historic/core districts and local markets.

todo.name: Museum and heritage stop
todo.desc: Anchor one cultural visit to understand local history and identity.

todo.name: Local food circuit
todo.desc: Plan a focused evening around regional specialties and neighborhood dining.

todo.name: Scenic viewpoint session
todo.desc: Schedule sunrise or sunset from a top city/nearby viewpoint.

todo.name: Nature or coast day trip
todo.desc: Reserve one full day for nearby nature, coast, or mountain access.

todo.name: Flexible slow day
todo.desc: Keep one low-structure day for weather pivots and spontaneous plans.

wifi.note: Urban hotels/cafés are usually stable; speeds can soften in outer districts or remote day-trip zones.

prac.fltNote: For central Tokyo stays and late arrivals, Haneda usually minimizes transfer time; Narita can price better on some long-haul dates but adds longer city transfers. In peak windows (late Mar-early Apr, Golden Week, Obon, late Dec-early Jan), aim to lock flights 4-7 months out and target hotels 3-6 months out; for major event weeks, 6+ months is safer.

airports.name: Tokyo Haneda Airport
airports.bestFor: First-time visitors, short breaks, and evening arrivals with simple city transfers
airports.transfer: Fastest access to central wards via Keikyu/Monorail + JR/Metro connections; late-night taxi costs are usually lower than from Narita.

airports.name: Narita International Airport
airports.bestFor: Price-led itineraries, specific airline schedules, or open-jaw/regional onward routing
airports.transfer: Skyliner and Narita Express are reliable but add time/cost; verify final train or limousine-bus cutoffs on late arrivals.

prac.lang: Japanese is the primary language; English support is best at transport hubs, hotels, and major attractions.
prac.tz: JST (UTC+9) year-round; no daylight saving time.
prac.visa: Japan entry is nationality-dependent; many short-stay tourists are visa-exempt, while others need a visa issued before departure.
alerts: During typhoon season (August to October), monitor JMA advisories and rail operator notices for potential same-day service suspensions.
For Golden Week, Obon, and New Year, reserve long-distance rail seats and key attractions 3-4 weeks ahead; if dates are fixed, book accommodation 4-6 months out.

Tokyo rail passes are not universally good value: they usually pay off only with frequent JR intercity/day-trip use. For mostly metro-based city days, IC card + point-to-point tickets is often cheaper and more flexible.
If flying out of Narita after evening events, confirm last train or limousine-bus times in advance since missed connections can require costly taxis or an airport hotel.

sweet: Typically, target the best weather-comfort shoulder months to balance conditions, crowds, and pricing.
